heading: Definitions
label: 46501 Definitions

L. 85–72672 Stat. 731Pub. L. 91–449, § 1(1)84 Stat. 921Pub. L. 93–36688 Stat. 409Pub. L. 95–163, § 17(b)(1)91 Stat. 1286Pub. L. 95–504, § 2(b)92 Stat. 1705Pub. L. 98–473, § 2013(c)98 Stat. 218949 App.:1472(n)(4)., , , § 902(n)(2), (4); added , , , 411.Aug. 23, 1958Aug. 5, 1974Pub. L. 85–72672 Stat. 731Pub. L. 93–366, § 103(b)88 Stat. 41046501(2)49 App.:1301(38) (words before 10th comma).46501(3)49 App.:1472(n)(2).In clause (2), before subclause (A), the words “any of the following” are substituted for “includes” for clarity. In subclause (B), the words “armed forces” are substituted for “national defense forces” because of 10:101. In subclause (D)(i), the word “place” is substituted for “point” for consistency in the revised title. The word “actually” is omitted as surplus. In subclause (D)(ii), the words “on which an individual commits” are substituted for “having . . . committed aboard” for clarity. In subclause (D)(iii), the words “against which an individual commits” are substituted for “regarding which an offense . . . is committed” for clarity. The words “(Montreal, )” are omitted as surplus. In subclause (E), the words “the lessee does not have a principal place of business” are substituted for “none” for clarity.September 23, 1971In clause (3), the words “by force or threat thereof, or . . . other” are omitted as surplus.

children.label: A
children.text: through the moment when one external door is opened to allow passengers to leave the aircraft; or

children.label: B
children.text: until, if a forced landing, competent authorities take over responsibility for the aircraft and individuals and property on the aircraft.

children.label: A
children.text: a civil aircraft of the United States.

children.label: B
children.text: an aircraft of the armed forces of the United States.

children.label: C
children.text: another aircraft in the United States.

children.label: i
children.text: that has its next scheduled destination or last place of departure in the United States, if the aircraft next lands in the United States;

children.label: ii
children.text: on which an individual commits an offense (as defined in the Convention for the Suppression of Unlawful Seizure of Aircraft) if the aircraft lands in the United States with the individual still on the aircraft; or

children.label: iii
children.text: against which an individual commits an offense (as defined in subsection (d) or (e) of article I, section I of the Convention for the Suppression of Unlawful Acts against the Safety of Civil Aviation) if the aircraft lands in the United States with the individual still on the aircraft.

children.label: E
children.text: any other aircraft leased without crew to a lessee whose principal place of business is in the United States or, if the lessee does not have a principal place of business, whose permanent residence is in the United States.

children.label: A
children.text: by any form of intimidation, unlawfully seizes, exercises control of, or attempts to seize or exercise control of, the aircraft; or

children.label: B
children.text: is an accomplice of an individual referred to in subclause (A) of this clause.
